title: Deployment Reproducibility Manifest v1
description: Records whether a public testnet deployment can be reproduced from public repository sources, and — when it cannot — the committed remediation path. A manifest may only declare reproducibilityStatus 'reproducible' when its own facts support it (reported commit in public history, matching runtime bytes, and an artifactManifest with a source tag + bytecode hash). Testnet only; this file never documents a mainnet deployment. Validated by `npm run validate:reproducibility`.

subject.description: The deployed contract name this manifest is about (e.g. 'WalletWallVault').

chainId.description: EVM chain ID. Testnets/local only; mainnet chain IDs are rejected by the validator.

reportedSourceCommit.description: The commit the deployment was reported to be built from.

reportedSourceCommitInPublicHistory.description: True only if reportedSourceCommit is actually present in public repository history.

observedRuntimeBytes.description: Byte length of the live deployed runtime bytecode.

publicHeadRuntimeBytes.description: Byte length of the runtime bytecode recompiled from public HEAD.

reproducibilityStatus.description: 'reproducible' is permitted only when the facts support it (see validator honesty cross-check).

remediation.description: Required when reproducibilityStatus is not 'reproducible'. Documents the concrete path to reproducibility.
